Update Video Path


Update the video file path for a creative asset.

This endpoint allows updating the S3 path for a creative's video file 
after the video has been uploaded or processed.

**Request Body:**
- `video_type`: Must be 'video'
- `video_path`: New S3 path for the video file

**Use Cases:**
- Update path after video transcoding
- Fix incorrect video paths
- Link processed videos to creative records

Get Top Game Categories


Get the top performing game categories based on campaign metrics.

Returns a ranked list of game categories by aggregate performance.
Results are cached for 24 hours.

**Response:**
- `count`: Number of categories in the list
- `data`: Array of category performance data

request.description:
Get Top Games List


Get the top performing games based on campaign metrics.

Returns a ranked list of games with their performance data and basic game information.
Results are cached for 24 hours.

**Query Parameters:**
- `by`: Metric to rank by (default: 'impressions')
- `start_date`: Start date for campaign data (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to 365 days ago.
- `end_date`: End date for campaign data (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today.

**Response:**
- `count`: Number of games in the list
- `data`: Array of game performance data with game details

Get Top IAB Categories


Get the top performing IAB categories based on campaign metrics.

Returns a ranked list of IAB content categories by aggregate performance.
Results are cached for 24 hours.

**Response:**
- `count`: Number of IAB categories in the list
- `data`: Array of IAB category performance data

ViewSet for managing Campaign Data records.

Campaign Data represents ad performance metrics (impressions, clicks, video completions, etc.)
aggregated by game, line item, date, country, ad format, exchange, and device type.

Batch Create Campaign Data


Batch create campaign data records from internal sources.

This internal endpoint allows batch creation of campaign data records.
Games and line items are created automatically if they don't exist.

**Request Body:**
- `records`: Array of campaign data objects containing:
  - `bundle_id`: Game bundle identifier
  - `line_item_id`: External line item ID
  - `network`: Ad network
  - `country`: Country code
  - `date`: Date of the data
  - `ad_format`: Ad format
  - `exchange`: Ad exchange
  - `device_type`: Device type
  - `ad_type`: Ad type
  - `impressions`, `clicks`, `ctr`, `vcr`, `cpm`, `spent`, `videos_completed`: Metrics

**Note:** This endpoint is restricted to internal requests only.

Batch Import from Firehose


Batch import campaign data records from AWS Kinesis Firehose.

This endpoint receives base64-encoded campaign data records from Firehose 
and processes them in bulk. Games and line items are created automatically 
if they don't exist.

**Request Body:**
- `records`: Array of base64-encoded campaign data objects
- `requestId`: Optional request ID for tracking
- `timestamp`: Optional timestamp

**Data Processing:**
- Decodes base64 records
- Creates/updates Games by bundle_id
- Creates/updates CampaignLineItems by external_id and network
- Creates/updates CampaignData with full metrics

**Note:** This endpoint is publicly accessible for Firehose integration.

request.description:
Cost Analysis Report


Comprehensive cost analysis report with multiple dimensions and metrics.

This endpoint generates a detailed cost analysis report supporting:
- **Multi-dimensional breakdowns**: network, country, ad_type, device_type, platform, exchange, genre, brand, agency, iab_category
- **Flexible time granularity**: yearly, monthly, or daily aggregation
- **Efficiency metrics**: CPC (Cost Per Click), CPVC (Cost Per Video Completion), eCPM
- **Budget utilization**: Spent vs budget analysis
- **Top performers**: Ranked lists by cost, impressions, and efficiency
- **Period-over-period variation**: Percentage changes between time periods

**Breakdown Options:**
- `network` - By ad network (Google, Loop Me, Chartboost, etc.)
- `country` - By country code
- `ad_type` - By ad type (interstitial, rewarded_video, banner, native)
- `device_type` - By device (phone, tablet, desktop)
- `platform` - By platform (iOS, Android)
- `exchange` - By ad exchange
- `genre` - By game genre
- `brand` - By brand name
- `agency` - By agency name
- `iab_category` - By IAB category

**Example Requests:**
- Monthly cost by network: `?group_by=month&breakdown_by=network`
- Daily cost by country: `?group_by=day&breakdown_by=country&start_date=2024-01-01&end_date=2024-01-31`
- Yearly cost with budget tracking: `?group_by=year&include_budget=true`

containing targeting, budget, and scheduling information.

Batch Sync from Airtable


Batch synchronize campaign line items from Airtable.

This internal endpoint syncs line item data from Airtable, creating or updating
line items and their associated projects.

**Request Body:**
- `records`: Array of line item objects containing:
  - `external_id`: External line item ID
  - `internal_id`: Internal identifier
  - `name`: Line item name
  - `network`: Ad network
  - `project`: Project internal ID
  - `is_playable`: Whether it's a playable ad
  - `status`: Campaign status
  - `budget`: Budget amount
  - `start_date`, `end_date`: Date range

**Note:** This endpoint is restricted to internal requests only.

Batch Create Line Items


Batch create or update campaign line items.

This internal endpoint allows bulk creation of line items.
Line items with duplicate external_id + network combinations will be updated.

**Request Body:**
- `records`: Array of line item objects

**Note:** This endpoint is restricted to internal requests only.

Batch Update by Field Value or Hash


Batch update line items matched by a classification field's current value or hash.

Use `field` + `value` or `field` + `hash` to identify which line items to update,
then provide the fields to change inside `update`.

**Request Body:**
- `records`: Array of objects, each containing:
  - `field` (required): The field to match on. One of: `ops_target`, `kpi`, `secondary_kpi`, `admazing_goal`.
  - `value` (optional): The exact current text value to match against.
  - `hash` (optional): The MD5 hash of the current value to match against.
  - `update` (required): An object of model fields to update on matched line items
    (e.g. `ops_target`, `kpi`, `secondary_kpi`, `admazing_goal`, `name`, `status`, `budget`, etc.)

You must provide either `value` or `hash` (or both) for each record.
Classification fields (`ops_target`, `kpi`, `secondary_kpi`, `admazing_goal`) are normalized before saving.

**Response:**
A list of results for each record, including the number of rows updated.

Sync All Line Items


Trigger a synchronization of all campaign line items from external sources.

This internal endpoint initiates a background process to fetch and update 
line item data from ad networks and DSPs.

**Note:** This endpoint is restricted to internal requests only.

Filter campaigns where ai_admazing_goal has any of the specified keys (comma-separated).

Filter campaigns where ai_admazing_goal has the specified top-level key.

► Examples:
  Find campaigns optimizing for VCR:
    → ?ai_admazing_goal__key=type=vcr

  Find campaigns with target value >= 85:
    → ?ai_admazing_goal__key=target__value__gte=85

  Find campaigns with target value = 90:
    → ?ai_admazing_goal__key=target__value=90

  Find campaigns with percent unit:
    → ?ai_admazing_goal__key=target__unit=percent

  Find campaigns with >= operator:

• Supports: key=value, key__subkey=value, key__subkey__gte=value, key__subkey__icontains=value
• Supports: Multiple conditions with AND logic (use same parameter multiple times)
• WARNING: Only works when ai_kpi_goal is an OBJECT, not an array.
• For array-format data, use __contains instead.

► Examples (for OBJECT format):

• Supports: key__subkey__has=value (for array fields - checks if array contains value)
• Supports: Multiple conditions with AND logic (use same parameter multiple times)

► Examples - Scalar values:
  Find campaigns targeting all genders:

  Find campaigns targeting females:

Filter campaigns where ai_target_audience.last_updated is >= the specified date.

• Format: ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS)

► Examples:

Filter campaigns where ai_target_audience.last_updated is <= the specified date.

• Format: ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS)

► Examples:

    → ?ai_target_audience__last_updated__lte=2025-12-31

  Find campaigns updated before a specific time:
    → ?ai_target_audience__last_updated__lte=2025-12-17T12:00:00

Filter campaigns where a nested value in ai_target_audience is null or not null.

• Format: path=true (is null) or path=false (is not null)
• Use double underscores (__) to access nested keys

► Examples:
  Find campaigns WITH country targeting (countries.value is not null):
    → ?ai_target_audience__nested_isnull=countries__value=false

  Find campaigns WITHOUT country targeting (countries.value is null):
    → ?ai_target_audience__nested_isnull=countries__value=true

  Find campaigns with interests defined:
    → ?ai_target_audience__nested_isnull=interests__value=false

  Find campaigns with min_age defined:
    → ?ai_target_audience__nested_isnull=min_age__value=false

  Find campaigns excluding certain genders:
    → ?ai_target_audience__nested_isnull=gender__excluded_value=false

Filter campaigns by ai_target_audience.version number (exact match).

► Examples:
  Find campaigns with version 0:
    → ?ai_target_audience__version=0

  Find campaigns with version 1:
    → ?ai_target_audience__version=1

Filter campaigns where ai_target_audience.version is >= the specified value.

► Examples:

request.description:
Get Campaign Analytics


Retrieve time-series analytics for a specific campaign.

This endpoint aggregates performance data from all line items associated with the campaign,
providing metrics over time with flexible grouping options.

**Date Range:**
If no dates are provided, the endpoint returns data for the **entire campaign lifetime** 
(from the earliest to the latest date with actual data).

**Metrics included:**
- `impressions`: Total ad impressions
- `clicks`: Total clicks
- `ctr`: Average click-through rate
- `vcr`: Average video completion rate
- `videos_completed`: Total video completions
- `cpm`: Average cost per mille
- `spent`: Total spend

**Grouping Options:**
- `day`: Daily aggregation (default)
- `week`: Weekly aggregation
- `month`: Monthly aggregation

**Available Breakdown Options:**
- `country`: Country (location)
- `ad_format`: Ad Format (ad)
- `device_type`: Device Type (device)
- `exchange`: Exchange (ad)
- `line_item__network`: Network (line_item)
- `game__name`: Game Name (game)
- `game__bundle_id`: Bundle ID (game)
- `game__platform`: Platform (game)
- `game__orientation`: Orientation (game)
- `game__genres`: Genres (game)

request.description:
Find Similar Campaigns


Find campaigns similar to this one using weighted fuzzy matching on AI-extracted data.

**Default Similarity Scoring (100 points total):**
- **Demographics** (28 points): Gender (14), Age range (14)
- **Geography** (22 points): Countries (18), Cities (4)
- **KPI Goals** (23 points): KPI type (14), KPI target (9)
- **IAB Category** (12 points): Industry classification match
- **Interests** (13 points)
- **Other** (2 points): Parental (1), Income (1), Marital (0)

**Customization:**
You can customize which fields are scored and their weights using the `fields` and `weights` parameters.
Scores are always normalized to 0-100 regardless of which fields/weights are used.

**Valid Fields:**
`gender`, `age_range`, `countries`, `cities`, `kpi_type`, `kpi_target`, `iab_category`,
`interests`, `parental_status`, `household_income`, `marital_status`

**Example Queries:**
- Default: `/campaigns/123/similar/`
- Custom weights: `/campaigns/123/similar/?weights={"gender":30,"countries":40,"kpi_type":30}`
- Specific fields: `/campaigns/123/similar/?fields=gender,countries,kpi_type`
- Both: `/campaigns/123/similar/?fields=gender,countries&weights={"gender":60,"countries":40}`

**Response includes:**
- `similarity_score`: Normalized score (0-100)
- `similarity_raw_score`: Raw score before normalization
- `similarity_breakdown`: Points breakdown by field
- `scoring_config`: Configuration used (fields, weights, max_raw_score)

Batch Create Projects


Create multiple campaigns in bulk from external sources (e.g., HubSpot).

This internal endpoint allows batch creation of campaigns with automatic deal linking.
Deals are created if they don't exist based on the HubSpot deal ID.

**Request Body:**
- `records`: Array of project objects containing:
  - `name`: Project name
  - `internal_id`: Unique internal identifier
  - `hs_deal_id`: Optional HubSpot deal ID for linking
  - `target`: Optional ops target
  - `kpi`: Optional primary KPI
  - `secondary_kpi`: Optional secondary KPI
  - `admazing_goal`: Optional Admazing goal

**Note:** This endpoint is restricted to internal requests only.

request.description:
Campaign Overview Report


Generate a comprehensive property-based overview report for campaigns, deals, and line items.

This report provides business intelligence on campaign properties rather than performance metrics,
including document completeness, AI data quality, budget allocation, and organizational breakdown.

**Available Filters:**
- `deal_id`: Filter by specific deal
- `start_date`: Filter campaigns starting on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `end_date`: Filter campaigns ending on or before this date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `brand_id`: Filter by brand
- `company_id`: Filter by company
- `agency_id`: Filter by agency
- `country`: Filter by deal country (ISO code, e.g., 'US', 'MX')
- `network`: Filter by ad network (google, admazing_dsp, loop_me, chartboost, facebook)
- `status`: Filter by line item status (pending, paused, running, ended, canceled)

**Report Sections:**
1. **Summary**: Total counts and date range
2. **Deal Documents**: Document completeness (briefs, proposals, media plans, IOs)
3. **AI Data Quality**: AI field population rates and certainty levels
4. **Deal Value**: Amount totals, PMP breakdown
5. **Budget Allocation**: Budget by network, format, status
6. **Target Audience**: Demographics from AI targeting data
7. **KPI Goals**: KPI type distribution and goal alignment
8. **IAB Categories**: Manual vs AI category distribution
9. **Line Items**: Network, format, status distributions
10. **Organizational**: Brands, companies, agencies, owners, countries

request.description:
Get Global Game Rankings


Retrieve a paginated list of games with their global ranking data including impressions, 
clicks, and video completion metrics.

This endpoint aggregates campaign performance data across all games and returns ranking 
information based on various metrics. Results are cached until the end of the month for 
performance optimization.

**Key Features:**
- Returns games with global performance metrics (impressions, clicks, CTR, VCR)
- Supports extensive filtering options (see filter parameters)
- Supports search by game name or bundle ID
- Results are cached for improved performance

**Global Metrics Included:**
- `global_impressions`: Total ad impressions across all campaigns
- `global_clicks`: Total ad clicks across all campaigns  
- `global_ctr`: Click-through rate (clicks/impressions)
- `global_vcr`: Video completion rate (for video ads)
- `global_vc`: Total video completions

request.description:
Export Global Ranking Data


Export the global ranking data to a file (CSV or other formats) and optionally send it via email.

This endpoint allows you to export filtered game ranking data for external analysis or reporting.
The export is processed asynchronously via a Lambda function, and the resulting file is stored in S3.

**Export Options (Request Body):**
- `format`: Export file format (default: 'csv')
- `fields`: List of fields to include in the export (default: ['bundle_id'])
- `emails`: List of email addresses to send the export to (internal emails only)
- `brand_list_id`: Optional - If provided, adds the exported games to the specified brand list

**Filtering (Query Parameters):**
All GamesFilter query parameters are supported to filter which games are exported.

**Limitations:**
- Maximum of 10,000 items per export
- Only internal email addresses (@admazing.co, @admazing.com, @thefastmind.com) are allowed

**Response includes:**
- `user_id`: The user who initiated the export
- `bundle_ids`: List of exported game bundle IDs
- `count`: Number of exported items
- `url`: URL to download the exported file (once ready)

request.description:
Get Global Ranking Statistics


Retrieve aggregated statistics for the global game rankings.

This endpoint provides summary statistics across all games matching the filter criteria,
including total impressions, clicks, downloads, reviews, and calculated metrics like CTR and VCR.

**Returned Statistics:**
- `count`: Number of games matching the filter criteria
- `total_impressions`: Sum of all ad impressions
- `total_clicks`: Sum of all ad clicks
- `total_views`: Sum of all video completions (only for rewarded video ad type)
- `total_downloads`: Sum of all game downloads
- `total_reviews`: Sum of all game reviews
- `total_ctr`: Overall click-through rate (total_clicks / total_impressions)
- `total_vcr`: Overall video completion rate (total_views / total_impressions)
- `average_rating`: Average game rating across filtered games

**Note:** Results are cached until the end of the month for performance optimization.

Batch Create Games


Create multiple game records in bulk using bundle IDs.

This internal endpoint allows batch creation of game records. If a game with the same 
bundle_id already exists, it will be updated instead of creating a duplicate.

**Request Body:**
- `records`: Array of game objects with at least `bundle_id` field

**Note:** This endpoint is restricted to internal requests only.

Batch Update Games


Update multiple game records in bulk.

This internal endpoint allows batch updating of game records. Games are matched by `bundle_id`.

**Request Body:**
- `records`: Array of game objects with `bundle_id` and fields to update
- `overwrite`: If true, overwrites all updatable fields; if false, only updates provided fields

**Genre Mapping:**
Genres provided in the records will be automatically mapped to valid genre choices.

**Note:** This endpoint is restricted to internal requests only.

Export Completed Callback


Callback endpoint triggered when a game export has completed.

This internal endpoint is called by the Lambda function after successfully exporting 
game data. It sends notification emails to specified recipients.

**Request Body:**
Contains export completion data including file URL and recipient emails.

**Note:** This endpoint is restricted to internal requests only.

Get All Bundle IDs


Retrieve a list of all game bundle IDs in the database.

This internal endpoint returns all unique bundle IDs without pagination.
Useful for data synchronization and validation purposes.

**Response:**
- `count`: Total number of bundle IDs
- `data`: Array of all bundle IDs

**Note:** This endpoint is restricted to internal requests only.

Reload Game Data


Refresh game metadata from external sources (App Store, Google Play).

This endpoint triggers a synchronous update of a specific game's metadata by 
fetching the latest data from the app store. The response includes the updated game data.

**Use Cases:**
- Force refresh when game metadata appears outdated
- Verify app store listing changes have propagated
- Debug data synchronization issues

**Note:** This is a synchronous operation that may take a few seconds to complete.

Flush Cached Data


Clear all cached data from the application cache (Memcache).

This endpoint clears all cached responses, including:
- Global ranking data
- Statistics
- Any other cached API responses

**Use Cases:**
- Force refresh after significant data updates
- Clear stale cache during debugging
- Reset cache after data imports

**Note:** In production, this endpoint is restricted to internal requests only.
In debug mode, it's publicly accessible.

Sync All Games


Trigger a synchronization of all games from external sources (App Store, Google Play).

This endpoint initiates a background process to fetch and update game metadata from app stores.
The sync can be run locally (for testing) or via Lambda function (for production).

**Request Body:**
- `local`: If true, runs the sync locally instead of via Lambda (default: false)

**Note:** This is typically used for scheduled maintenance or initial data population.

Update Content Sensitivity


Trigger the content sensitivity analysis update for all games.

This endpoint invokes the `updateContentSensitivity` Lambda function which will
analyze and update the `ai_content_sensitivity` field for all games in the database.

**Process:**
- Triggers an asynchronous Lambda function
- The Lambda function processes all games and updates their content sensitivity data
- Results are stored in the `ai_content_sensitivity` field on each game record

**Use Cases:**
- Refresh content sensitivity data after algorithm updates
- Periodic refresh of content analysis
- Initial population of content sensitivity data

**Note:** This endpoint is restricted to admin users only.

request.description:
Get Overall Statistics


Retrieve overall aggregated statistics for all games and campaign data.

This endpoint provides summary statistics across all campaign data (not filtered by game-specific 
global ranking like `global_ranking_stats`). It aggregates data directly from the CampaignData model.

**Query Parameters:**
- `start_date`: Start date for campaign data (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to 365 days ago for performance.
- `end_date`: End date for campaign data (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today.

**Returned Statistics:**
- `count`: Number of games matching the filter criteria
- `total_impressions`: Sum of all ad impressions from CampaignData
- `total_clicks`: Sum of all ad clicks from CampaignData
- `total_views`: Sum of all video completions from CampaignData
- `total_downloads`: Sum of all game downloads
- `total_reviews`: Sum of all game reviews
- `total_ctr`: Overall click-through rate (clicks / impressions)
- `total_vcr`: Video completion rate (video completions / video ad impressions only)
- `average_rating`: Average game rating

**Note:** Results are cached until the end of the month. VCR is calculated using only 
rewarded video ad impressions for accuracy, not diluted by non-video ad types.
